title: Braised Pork Belly with Fermented Chili
description: A Chinese-inspired dish that combines the richness of pork belly with the depth of fermented chili. Perfect for a comforting and flavorful meal.

ingredients.name: pork belly (2 lbs)
ingredients.notes: Score the fat in a crisscross pattern

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.notes: Can add more to achieve braising liquid consistency

ingredients.name: soy sauce
ingredients.notes: Can add more to enhance flavor

ingredients.name: sugar
ingredients.notes: Optional for balance of sweet and savory

instructions: In a large Dutch oven or heavy pot, combine pork belly, fermented chili paste, tender shoots, chinese rice vinegar, water, soy sauce, and sugar.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the pork belly for 2 hours or until it reaches an internal temperature of 160°F (71°C).
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Using two forks, shred the pork belly into tender pieces.
Strain the braising liquid and discard the solids.
Return the shredded pork belly to the pot and simmer in the braising liquid for an additional 10-15 minutes or until heated through.
Serve the braised pork belly with the braising liquid spooned over the top and garnished with tender shoots.
